chrome.purpose: Header, brand, and shell chrome. Josefin Sans in Emerald; each theme may substitute its own bundled family.

label.purpose: Body copy, buttons, menu items, channel labels. The workhorse role — 13px is the floor for action labels.

mono.purpose: Anything the audio engine measured: timer, bar numbers, CH ordinals, dB readouts, FX slot summaries, LCD text.

label-small.purpose: Lane names (11px) and the densest chrome. Truncates with ellipsis plus a tooltip.

shadows.name: shadow-mixer-panel
shadows.purpose: Channel-bank and FX-bank slabs lifting off the Mixer device surface.

shadows.name: shadow-mixer-slot
shadows.purpose: Channel strips and FX cards. Flat in Emerald; other themes may bevel or emboss.

shadows.name: shadow-mixer-led
shadows.purpose: Glow behind status and FX power LEDs. currentColor makes each LED glow in its own slot accent.

shadows.name: shadow-transport
shadows.purpose: Idle transport button. The only chrome family permitted a resting shadow.

shadows.name: shadow-sample-bubble-text
shadows.purpose: Applied only when the computed bubble ink is white.

shadows.name: shadow-sample-bubble
shadows.purpose: Canvas-parsed as "<x>px <y>px <blur>px <color>" or "none". Must match the DOM bubble exactly.

shadows.name: shadow-lane
shadows.purpose: Inset well shadow on the lane placement area.

shadows.name: shadow-pill
shadows.purpose: Pill-family chrome. Flat by doctrine in Emerald.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: The single focus-visible treatment across the app. Never removed, never restyled per component.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Border and background color shifts on hover/focus chrome.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Default for background and transform state transitions. Product UI stays in the 150-250ms band.

motion.name: flash-attention
motion.purpose: Locate-in-browser bubble flash. Replaced by a static indicator under reduced motion.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: prefers-reduced-motion: reduce removes transitions and swaps the scan spinner and locate flash for static indicators.

breakpoints.name: minimum
breakpoints.purpose: Renderer content minimum. Below either dimension the app shows only the unsupported-resolution notice.

breakpoints.name: ui-size-30
breakpoints.purpose: Default density. Header 48px, lane 37px, bubble 24px, channel strip 76px.

components.name: Transport Button (Active)
components.description: The one filled accent action on any surface. Play is accent-colored when stopped, Pause when playing.
components.html: <button class="ds-transport ds-transport-active" aria-label="Pause"><svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 14 14" fill="currentColor" aria-hidden="true"><rect x="2" y="1" width="4" height="12" rx="1"/><rect x="8" y="1" width="4" height="12" rx="1"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-transport { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 30px; height: 30px; border: none; border-radius: var(--radius-transport, 8px); background: var(--gradient-transport, linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.07), rgba(0,0,0,0.16))), var(--transport, #0C2D32); color: var(--on-transport, #E8F0EC); box-shadow: var(--shadow-transport, 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)); cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease; } .ds-transport-active { background: var(--gradient-transport-active, linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.12), rgba(0,0,0,0.12))), var(--transport-active, #00674F); color: var(--on-transport-active, #E8F0EC); box-shadow: var(--shadow-transport-active, 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)); } .ds-transport:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: The default for every non-transport command. Transparent at rest; earns an accent-tinted surface only on hover, focus-visible, or active.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { min-height: 30px; padding: 0 10px; border: var(--border-width, 1px) solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius, 0.22rem); background: transparent; color: var(--text-muted, #B8D0C8); font-family: var(--font-label, 'Ubuntu', sans-serif); font-size: 13px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 120ms ease, background-color 120ms ease, color 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: var(--pill-bg, #0C2D32); border-color: var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); color: var(--text, #E8F0EC); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); outline-offset: 2px; border-color: var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); } .ds-btn-ghost:disabled { opacity: 0.45; cursor: not-allowed; background: transparent; border-color: transparent; }

components.name: Pill
components.description: Quiet grouped chrome — status readouts and grouped controls sharing one rounded surface rather than each rendering as a raised slab.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 30px; padding: 0 10px; border: var(--border-width-pill, 1px) solid var(--pill-border, #2D6B5E); border-radius: var(--radius, 0.22rem); background: var(--pill-bg, #0C2D32); color: var(--text, #E8F0EC); font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 12px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-pill, none); }

components.name: Sample Bubble
components.description: The signature component. Identical in the Tracker canvas, browser grid, and drag image — same height, width, and treatment everywhere.
components.css: .ds-bubble { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 24px; padding: 0 8px; border-radius: var(--radius-sample-bubble, 6px); background: var(--palette-0, #982A00); color: var(--palette-ink-0, #FFFFFF); font-family: var(--font-label, 'Ubuntu', sans-serif); font-size: 11px; font-weight: var(--sample-bubble-font-weight, 400); text-transform: var(--sample-bubble-case, none); text-shadow: var(--palette-shadow-0, 1.5px 1.5px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.55)); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sample-bubble, none); white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; max-width: 220px; cursor: grab; } .ds-bubble:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Sample Bubble (Selected)
components.description: Selection state. The bubble keeps its full size and data; only the fill changes.

components.name: Rotary Control
components.description: Shared project-owned SVG dial: 270-degree track, high-contrast value arc, inset cap, short pointer. Used for sends, returns, pan, FX params, and rack knobs. A CSS-only circle is never a substitute.
components.html: 64%
components.css: .ds-rotary { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 2px; cursor: ns-resize; touch-action: none; } .ds-rotary-value { font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-muted, #B8D0C8); } .ds-rotary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: var(--radius, 0.22rem); }

components.name: Linear Fader
components.description: The canonical LinearSlider visual: one recessed rectangular rail, accent value fill, low-profile rectangular hardware handle. Horizontal sliders rotate this same geometry — never a circle.
components.css: .ds-fader { display: inline-flex; justify-content: center; width: 30px; height: 140px; cursor: ns-resize; touch-action: none; } .ds-fader-rail { position: relative; width: 8px; height: 100%; border-radius: 2px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.45); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.6); } .ds-fader-fill { position: absolute; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; height: 80%; border-radius: 2px; background: var(--accent, #00674F); } .ds-fader-thumb { position: absolute; left: 50%; bottom: 80%; transform: translate(-50%, 50%); width: 22px; height: 10px; border-radius: 2px; background: var(--fill-mixer-knob, #063A2B); border: 1px solid var(--border-mixer-knob, #167A56); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); } .ds-fader:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: var(--radius, 0.22rem); }

components.name: FX Slot
components.description: One of four combined FX and Return containers in a fixed 2x2 grid. Header carries slot number, module name, and a power LED that is itself the toggle.
components.css: .ds-fx-slot { display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-between; width: 160px; height: 112px; padding: 6px 8px; border: var(--border-width, 1px) solid var(--border, #1A4D3E); border-radius: var(--radius, 0.22rem); background: var(--gradient-mixer-panel, none), var(--bg-panel, #051411); box-shadow: var(--shadow-mixer-slot, none); color: var(--text, #E8F0EC); font-family: var(--font-label, 'Ubuntu', sans-serif); } .ds-fx-head { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; } .ds-fx-num { font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-muted, #B8D0C8); } .ds-fx-name { flex: 1; font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-fx-led { width: 20px; height: 20px; padding: 0; border: none; background: none; color: var(--fx-accent-1, #22B573); cursor: pointer; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-fx-led::after { content: ''; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: currentColor; box-shadow: var(--shadow-mixer-led, 0 0 5px rgba(34,181,115,0.6)); } .ds-fx-led[aria-pressed='false']::after { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.18); box-shadow: none; } .ds-fx-body { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-fx-edit { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 24px; height: 24px; border: var(--border-width, 1px) solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius, 0.22rem); background: transparent; color: var(--fx-accent-1, #22B573); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; } .ds-fx-edit:hover { background: var(--pill-bg, #0C2D32); border-color: var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); } .ds-fx-limiter { width: 20px; height: 20px; border: 1px solid var(--pill-border, #2D6B5E); border-radius: 2px; background: var(--pill-bg, #0C2D32); color: var(--text, #E8F0EC); font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 10px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-fx-limiter[aria-pressed='false'] { opacity: 0.5; } .ds-fx-foot { font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 10px; color: var(--text-muted, #B8D0C8); white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-fx-slot:focus-within { outline: 2px solid var(--highlight, #8FBCB2); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Panel Header
components.description: The shared panel header grammar: small uppercase mono count on the left, status LED plus state on the right.
components.html: 4 × FX SlotsActive
components.css: .ds-panel { padding: 8px; border: var(--border-width, 1px) solid var(--border, #1A4D3E); border-radius: var(--radius, 0.22rem); background: var(--gradient-mixer-panel, none), var(--bg-panel, #051411); box-shadow: var(--shadow-mixer-panel, 0 8px 20px rgba(0,10,6,0.55)); } .ds-panel-head { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-muted, #B8D0C8); } .ds-panel-state { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; color: var(--fx-accent-1, #22B573); } .ds-panel-led { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: currentColor; box-shadow: var(--shadow-mixer-led, 0 0 5px rgba(34,181,115,0.6)); }

components.name: Rack Module Faceplate
components.description: One of thirteen Master Bus modules. Fixed hardware finish scoped to the rack — the deliberate exception to the token rule.
components.html:
03
SAT
Tube Saturation
components.css: .ds-rack-module { width: 152px; padding: 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.5); border-radius: 6px; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #71413a, #5c332d); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.14); color: #f4e8de; font-family: var(--font-label, 'Ubuntu', sans-serif); } .ds-rack-top { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; } .ds-rack-ord { font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 11px; opacity: 0.75; } .ds-rack-power { width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 50%; background: #f4c14f; box-shadow: 0 0 6px rgba(244,193,79,0.7); } .ds-rack-chip { display: inline-block; margin-top: 8px; padding: 1px 6px; border-radius: 2px; background: #ff8a4e; color: #2b1408; font-family: var(--font-mono, 'JetBrains Mono', monospace); font-size: 9px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-rack-name { margin-top: 4px; font-size: 13px; }

narrative.overview:
MixJam is not a website that plays audio. It is a piece of gear. The Master tab holds thirteen module faceplates in a screwed-down rack shell; the Mixer holds channel strips and FX slots on a device surface; knobs have real pointers and value arcs, LEDs glow when a bus is live, faders ride in recessed rails. Depth in this interface is hardware depth, never decoration. When a surface lifts off the page, it is because it represents something you could put your hands on.

The second half of the identity is that the hardware is skinnable. Sixteen themes ship in public/themes/, and each one repaints the entire instrument — surfaces, accents, three font roles, gradients, shadows, knob faces, corner radii — without moving a single control. Geometry is fixed and shared; identity is entirely token-driven. Emerald (deep green, dark) is the default, but the system is designed so that Mono's single phosphor green and Riso's print palette are equally native, not skins bolted onto a green app.

This system explicitly rejects the SaaS dashboard: no metric-card grids, no gradients-for-the-sake-of-gradients, no glassmorphism, no corporate sheen. It also rejects full-DAW density theater — the eJay / Sony Acid tracker model is deliberately simple, and the chrome must stay quiet enough that a 100,000-sample library still feels instant. The tool disappears into the task.
keyCharacteristics: Dark by default; light themes (Vintage, Soft, Riso) are first-class, not afterthoughts.
Every semantic color is a CSS custom property. Hardcoded hex is a defect.
One primary accent action per surface. Everything else is quiet or ghost.
Continuous surface: related controls share a rounded group background rather than each rendering as a raised bordered slab.
Three font roles (chrome / label / mono), zero system-font fallbacks.
Fixed pixel geometry at three UI Sizes (30 / 40 / 50), never fluid typography.
The root viewport never scrolls. Internal panels do.

rules.name: The Token Rule
rules.body: Every semantic color comes from a CSS custom property. Local literals are permitted in exactly three places: invariant neutral overlays, selection ink, and canvas safety fallbacks. A hex code anywhere else is a bug, and it will break fifteen of the sixteen themes.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Play/Pause is the sole filled accent action on any surface. Every other command is quiet or ghost, earning an accent-tinted surface only on hover, focus-visible, or active. If two things on a screen are shouting in accent, one of them is wrong.

rules.name: The Hardware Exception Rule
rules.body: The Master Bus rack is fixed hardware. Its faceplate finishes, family chip colors, and meter faces are constants scoped to the rack, exactly like physical gear. Everything around the rack — panel chrome, scrollbars, focus rings, text outside faceplates — stays token-driven. The exception is the rack, not a license.

rules.name: The No-Fallback Rule
rules.body: Every visible glyph — labels, buttons, links, chrome, status text, tooltips, menu items, placeholder text, input values — must resolve to a bundled theme font through a font-family token. If any element renders in a system font or browser default, the theme is broken. Placeholder text is the one people forget.

rules.name: The Measured-Value Rule
rules.body: If a number represents something the audio engine measured — time, level, tempo, position, gain reduction — it renders in mono. If it is a label, it does not.

rules.name: The No-Raised-Idle Rule
rules.body: An idle button never renders as a raised bordered slab. Related controls share one subtle rounded group background instead. If a row of buttons reads as a row of separate chips at rest, regroup it.

rules.name: The Canvas-Parity Rule
rules.body: The lane canvas parses --shadow-sample-bubble, --border-sample-bubble, and --gradient-sample-bubble as strings in fixed formats. A bubble must look identical in the Tracker canvas, the browser grid, and the drag image. Change one, change all three.

rules.name: The Shared-Primitive Rule
rules.body: If a control already exists as a project-owned primitive — LinearSlider, the rotary SVG, the Dialog — a feature must use it. Assembling a one-off from Radix parts or CSS is prohibited, no matter how small the difference seems.

rules.name: The Disjoint-Rectangle Rule
rules.body: No interactive container may overlap another. Every hit-testable rectangle is disjoint: no z-index fights, no invisible catch-basins over other controls, no stacked interactive surfaces sharing pixels.

dos: Do read every color from a theme token. Add a new semantic need to ThemeColors and to all sixteen theme JSONs, then use it.
Do keep the root viewport free of scrollbars. Every shell view fits 1920x1080 exactly; internal panels (lane list, browser grid, Mixer strips, FX containers) scroll instead.
Do use fixed pixel geometry from the UI Size tokens (30 / 40 / 50). Square controls take the token exactly; text-bearing controls take it as a minimum cross-axis size.
Do hold transitions to 150-250ms. Users are in flow.
Do give every interactive component its full state set: default, hover, focus-visible, active, disabled, and where applicable loading, selected, and bypassed.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — the scan spinner and locate-in-browser flash become static indicators and transitions are removed.
Do verify a change against all sixteen themes, light ones included. Vintage, Soft, and Riso are light; a change that only reads on dark is unfinished.
Do keep --text-muted genuinely readable. Muted is a hierarchy signal, not a license for low contrast.
donts: Don't hardcode a color, shadow, gradient, or radius. Invariant neutral overlays, selection ink, and canvas safety fallbacks are the only exceptions.
Don't build the SaaS-dashboard aesthetic PRODUCT.md rejects by name: no metric-card grids, no gradients-for-the-sake-of-gradients, no glassmorphism, no corporate sheen.
Don't reach for full-DAW complexity. No piano rolls, no automation lanes, no plugin hosting. The eJay / Sony Acid model is the point.
Don't render idle buttons as raised bordered slabs. Group them on one shared rounded surface.
Don't put a second filled accent action on a surface that already has Play/Pause.
Don't let any element fall back to a system font — placeholder text and input values included.
Don't use fluid typography. No clamp() headings; this is product UI at a fixed minimum resolution.
Don't overlap interactive containers or fight with z-index.
Don't invent hardware that no spec calls for. The reference board governs structure and density only — no invented screws, tape labels, or fake wear on the Mixer.
Don't apply full-saturation accent to inactive or disabled states.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on any card, list item, or callout.
Don't animate for decoration. Motion here conveys state change, feedback, loading, or reveal — nothing else.
